title: Encrypted Inquiry — PlausiDen LLC
description: Encrypted intake channel for journalism, healthcare, finance, legal, and other threat models that warrant end-to-end encrypted communication.

footer.colophon: © 2026 PlausiDen LLC. Reproducible builds. Signed audit chains. No third-party trackers.

sections.title: End-to-end encrypted intake.
sections.lede: For threat models that warrant it: journalism source-protection, healthcare PHI scoping, finance privileged-information channels, legal client work-product. We accept inquiries via age, PGP, Signal, or Tor hidden service.

background.alt: Wide editorial photograph evoking secure communication

items.definition: Encrypt with our age public key. We publish the key fingerprint in three places (this page, a signed Git tag in our public repo, an OpenTimestamps proof) so you can verify before encrypting. age-encrypted files attach to any email account.
Traditional encryption channel. Our OpenPGP key is published on keys.openpgp.org and verified there. Use any GnuPG-compatible client. We respond signed; verify our signature before acting on advice.
Encrypted messaging. Best for time-sensitive inquiries and ongoing engagement coordination. Request the number via age- or PGP-encrypted email; we do not publish the number on the public web.
A .onion address for clients who require the strongest network-layer anonymity. Same intake process as the clearnet channels; the network path is the only difference.

sections.body: If the threat model warrants encryption, the threat model warrants verifying every key before use. Three independent verification paths; pick at least two.

sections.text: Verify before you encrypt. Each key is published on at least three independent channels — this page, a signed Git tag in our public repository, and an OpenTimestamps proof referencing a Bitcoin block. If any two diverge, do not encrypt — the key is compromised.

items.value: Published at /security/age.pub (signed). Fingerprint posted in latest GitHub release notes.
Published at /security/pgp.asc (ASCII-armored). Also on keys.openpgp.org under team@plausiden.com.
Published on request via age or PGP. Rotated quarterly.

sections.caption: Verify keys via multiple independent paths before encrypting.

sections.title: If your threat model doesn't warrant encryption,
sections.lede: use the regular contact page. Plain email and phone are perfectly fine for routine inquiry.
